game_name: Zelda II: The Adventure of Link

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player starts with sword; early weak enemies teach timing
Gradual progression through 7 levels per attribute; permanent upgrades in Western version
Learned from NPCs; costs magic meter resource; enables puzzle and combat solutions
Essential for specific enemy types and secret passages
Crystal collection gates narrative; later palaces demand higher skill
Provide hints, spells, and narrative progression clues

element_introduction_order.element: Encounter system (random blob/biped icons on overworld)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Small blob = weak enemies; large biped = harder enemies; player can avoid or engage
Relief mechanic; appears randomly to restore resources
Proximity continue; losing all lives resets progress (attribute levels reset to lowest in JP version)
Scattered across world; player acquires up to 4 additional containers beyond starting 4

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension: overworld traversal (moderate threat) → palace entry (sharp escalation) → boss fight (high tension) → crystal gain (relief, story progression). Encounter system allows player agency in tension management.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: NPCs in towns hint at secret passages and optional spell locations; exploration rewards experience and item discovery
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Down-thrust attacks reveal hidden passages and secrets; palaces contain maze-like layouts with optional areas

difficulty_curve.early_game: Parapa Palace introduces basic combat and palace traversal; weak enemies teach mechanics; player starts with modest stats (4 heart containers, 4 magic containers)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Mid-tier palaces (Maze, Drop, Parody) introduce complex layouts, tougher enemy combinations, and spell-gating; player has accrued 2-3 attribute levels
difficulty_curve.late_game: Final palaces (Hidden Palace) demand high attribute levels and spell mastery; boss encounters are substantially harder; game requires optimized builds
notable_spikes: Transition from overworld to first palace (side-scroller perspective shift)
Mid-game palace complexity spike (Maze Palace labyrinth design)
Dark Link boss fight (mirrors player's current stats; escalates based on progression)
Final palace enemy density and hazard concentration

notable_level_patterns.description: Six dungeons arranged across overworld; each palace guards a crystal; later palaces increase in spatial complexity and enemy toughness; boss fight concludes each palace
Top-down towns scatter across overworld; contain NPCs offering spells, hints, and narrative; serve as relief points between hostile encounters
Dynamic random battles triggered by movement through overworld tiles; player sees icon type (blob/biped/Fairy) before engagement; allows risk assessment and route planning
Complex interconnected rooms with multiple exits; require navigation skill and spell use to progress; secret areas reward exploration
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Maze Palace (4th dungeon); Drop Palace vertical design
